title: That even a baptized person loses the grace that he has attained, unless he keep innocency.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: That even a baptized person loses the grace that he has attained, unless he keep innocency.

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: In the Gospel according to John: “Lo, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing happen unto thee.”

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Also in the first Epistle of Paul to the Corinthians: “Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and the Spirit of God abideth in you?

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: If any one violate the temple of God, him will God destroy.”

verses.book: Three Books of Testimonies Against the Jews.
verses.text: Of this same thing in the Chronicles: “God is with you, while ye are with Him: if ye forsake Him, He will forsake you.”
